original_abstract: U.S. government research into the health consequences of nuclear radiation involved systematic violations of the human rights of the Marshallese people. Evidence suggests that radioactive fallout from Pacific weapons testing was likely deposited across a much broader range of Micronesian islands than officially acknowledged. To rectify this history of opacity, the U.S. government must declassify and release all data regarding testing protocols, fallout patterns, and the subsequent health impacts on the populations of the Marshall Islands and greater Micronesia. Furthermore, the medical community has a professional and ethical responsibility to hold nuclear-armed states accountable to their treaty obligations regarding the total elimination of these weapons. Finally, as an urgent matter of social justice and to uphold the fundamental human right to health, the federal government should restore Medicaid eligibility for all Micronesian migrants living in the U.S. under the Compact of Free Association.
humanized_abstract: The health effects of ionizing radiation were the subject of some of the most extensive research by the US government. That research was obviously conducted with the utmost disregard for the human rights of the Marshallese. Recent work suggests that there was almost certainly more radioactive fallout than the US has acknowledged deposited on a wider area of Micronesia. We believe that the results of the testing and all related information (including the exact nature of all the tests that were carried out, the projected paths and patterns of any radioactive fallout that was released during these tests, and any research that has been carried out on the effect of this radiation on the human population of the Marshall Islands and other areas of Micronesia) should be made public. This requires the declassification of all government documents pertaining to the testing program and the effects of the radiation on inhabitants of the region. Doctors have a professional and moral obligation to oppose any policies that facilitate the continued development of nuclear arms, as the use of such weapons is directly at odds with the international obligations of states that agree to abide by the Treaty for the Prohibition of Nuclear Weapons in a Nuclear-Free Zone, which requires the elimination of such arms. We urge the federal government to restore Medicaid to all compact residents in the US. This is a matter of great urgency and an issue of social justice and international human rights: the right to health is a fundamental right.
